memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Franklyn, Francklyn, Francklin, Franklin, Franklind and many more. First found in Buckinghamshire where they were seated from very ancient times, some say well before the Norman Conquest and the arrival of Duke William at Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Henry Franklin who settled in Virginia in 1635; Thomas Franklin settled in New England in 1679; Josiah Franklin settled in Boston Mass. in 1630; William Franklin settled in Massachusetts in 1633. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: For King and country. This interesting old status name has its origins in the feudal system of medieval England. It derives from the Middle English "frankelin", from the Anglo-Norman French "franc" meaning free, in the sense of liberal and generous, plus the germanic suffix "ling". The status of the Franklin varied somewhat according to time and place in medieval England; in general, he was a free man and a holder of fairly extensive areas of land, a gentleman ranked above the main body of minor freeholders, but below a knight or a member of the nobility. The surname dates back to the late 12th Century. One, Luke le Franckeleyn is registered in the Cambridgeshire Feet of Fines (1234). In the modern idiom the name has six spelling variations, Frankling, Francklyn, Francklin, Franklen, Franklyn and Franklin. On 11 February 1560, George Franklin married Agnes Hills, at St. Dionis Backchurch, London. The American statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin (1706-1790), was of British descent. His father, a maker of soap and candles, had emigrated in about 1682, from Ecton, Northamptonshire, to Boston, where his son was born. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Ralph Frankelein, which was dated 1195, in the Pipe Rolls of Yorkshire, during the reign of King Richard 1st, known as "The Lionheart", 1189 - 1199. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.East Kennett, Wiltshire, England: 英國維爾爾易亞斯特 肯尼特
zh.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin於1524年出生在英國奧可福爾比斯特。他於1549年11月30日與Katherine or Catherine Denryte在英國奧可福爾比斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1594年11月30日在他的家鄉享年70歲。

zh-hans.East Kennett, Wiltshire, England: 英国維爾爾易亞斯特 肯尼特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin于1524年出生在英国奧可福爾比斯特。他于1549年11月30日与Katherine or Catherine Denryte在英国奧可福爾比斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1594年11月30日在他的家乡享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Franklin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman, freeholder, land-owner of free but not noble birth’ (Anglo-Norman French fraunclein). In general, he was a gentleman ranked above the main body of minor freeholders but below a knight or a member of the nobility.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames, such as   Frankel  .

History: The American statesman and scientist Benjamin Franklin (1706–90) was the son of Josiah Franklin, a chandler (dealer in soap and candles), who had emigrated c. 1682 from Ecton, Northamptonshire, to Boston, MA, where his son was born.

variants.name: Frankland
meanings: English: probably a variant of Franklin , with excrescent -d, a status name from Middle English frankeleyn ‘freeman, freeholder, land-owner of free but not noble birth’ (Anglo-Norman French fraunclein) for someone who lived on a piece of land held without obligations of rent or service. Compare Freeland .

variants.name: Frankel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Moshe, Benzion, Hyman, Leibish, Mayer, Meyer, Nachum, Yitzchok, Ari, Chaim, Dov.
German (mainly Fränkel) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Fränkel): variant of Frankl . Compare Fraenkel .
History: This is one of the old Ashkenazic surnames. In Eastern Europe, it was used by rabbinical families well before the mass adoption of surnames by local Jews at the turn of the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin was born in 1524, in Bicester, Oxfordshire, England. He married Katherine or Catherine Denryte on 30 November 1549, in Bicester, Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 30 November 1594, in his hometown, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin est né en 1524, à Bicester, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Katherine or Catherine Denryte le 30 novembre 1549, à Bicester, Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 30 novembre 1594, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin wurde 1524 in Bicester, Oxfordshire, England geboren. Er hat am 30. November 1549 in Bicester, Oxfordshire, England Katherine or Catherine Denryte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 30. November 1594 im Alter von 70 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin è nato il nel 1524 nel Bicester, Oxfordshire, England. Lui sposato Katherine or Catherine Denryte il 30 novembre 1549, nel Bicester, Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 30 novembre 1594, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.Bicester, Oxfordshire, England: イギリスオクスフォードシュアイアビセスター
ja.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklinは1524年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアビセスターで生まれた。1549年11月30日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアビセスターでKatherine or Catherine Denryteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1594年11月30日に故郷で70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin(은)는 1524년 영국 스퍼드실르 바이스스터에서 태어났다. 그는 1549년 11월 30일 영국 스퍼드실르 바이스스터에서 Katherine or Catherine Denryte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1594년 11월 30일 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin nasceu em 1524, em Bicester, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Katherine or Catherine Denryte em 30 de novembro de 1549, em Bicester, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de novembro de 1594, em sua cidade natal, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin родился в 1524 г. в местности баисестир, оксфордшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine or Catherine Denryte 30 ноября 1549 г. в местности баисестир, оксфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 30 ноября 1594 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William Richard Franklin nació en 1524, en Bicester, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Katherine or Catherine Denryte el 30 de noviembre de 1549, en Bicester, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 30 de noviembre de 1594, en su pueblo, a la edad de 70 años.
